title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Array References

statement.markdown: Given a memoized React component that re-renders excessively due to changing array references, optimize the component's re-rendering by correctly using the `useMemo` hook to memoize the array reference.
statement.inputFormat: Array of objects `[{id: number, name: string}, {id: number, name: string}]`
statement.notes: The component should re-render only when the array reference changes.

examples.explanation: The array reference is the same, so the component should not re-render.
The array reference is the same, so the component should not re-render.
The array reference is different, so the component should re-render.

hints.text: Use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the array reference.

editorial.title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Array References
editorial.intuition: The `useMemo` hook can be used to memoize the array reference, preventing the component from re-rendering excessively.
editorial.bruteForce: Creating a new array reference on every render can lead to excessive re-renders.
editorial.betterSolution: Using `useMemo` can prevent excessive re-renders by memoizing the array reference.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use `useMemo` with the correct dependency array to memoize the array reference.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correct implementation should re-render only when the array reference changes.
